headline: Cycling Infrastructure Covers Only 480km Across Malaysia
context: Malaysia has only 480km of dedicated cycling lanes nationwide compared to 1,800km in Singapore.

cards.big: Malaysia has only 480km cycling lanes versus Singapore 1,800km
cards.sub: Cycling mode share remains below 1% in all major Malaysian cities

cards.big: KL built 62km of bike lanes since 2018 but 40km are disconnected segments
cards.sub: Cyclists cannot travel safely from any residential area to city centre

cards.big: Penang allocated RM120 million for 200km cycling network by 2028
cards.sub: George Town pilot achieved 3.2% cycling mode share highest in Malaysia

cards.big: National cycling deaths rose to 180 in 2025 with 92% on roads without lanes
cards.sub: MIROS classified Malaysian roads as extremely hostile to cycling regionally

cards.big: Cycling gap tests whether sustainable transport receives real infrastructure commitment
480km of cycling infrastructure nationwide — meaningful modal shift requires a network, not isolated segments.
